volumeInfo.title: Norwegian Wood
volumeInfo.description: A nostalgic story of loss and sexuality set in 1960s Japan.

volumeInfo.title: Convenience Store Woman
volumeInfo.description: A haunting, dark, and often hilarious take on society's expectations of the single woman' Elif Batuman, author of The Idiot

volumeInfo.title: There's no such thing as an easy job
volumeInfo.description: A young woman walks into an employment agency and requests a job that has the following traits: it is close to her home, and it requires no reading, no writing – and ideally, very little thinking.

volumeInfo.title: Dom Casmurro
volumeInfo.description: A psychological novel exploring jealousy and memory in Brazil.

volumeInfo.title: The Hour of the Star
volumeInfo.description: A minimalist novel about a poor young woman in Rio de Janeiro.

volumeInfo.title: Crooked Plow
volumeInfo.description: Heralded as the most important Brazilian novel of the century so far, this bestseller's unique blend of magic and social realism won it three literary awards and global acclaim.

volumeInfo.title: The Vegetarian
volumeInfo.description: A haunting story of rebellion and transformation.

volumeInfo.title: Please Look After Mom
volumeInfo.description: A family reflects on the disappearance of their mother.

volumeInfo.title: Cursed Bunny
volumeInfo.description: Blurring the lines between magical realism, horror, and science-fiction, Chung uses elements of the fantastic and surreal to address the very real horrors and cruelties of patriarchy and capitalism in modern society.

volumeInfo.title: The Cairo Trilogy
volumeInfo.description: A sweeping saga of three generations in Cairo.

volumeInfo.title: Season of Migration to the North
volumeInfo.description: A postcolonial exploration of identity and displacement.

volumeInfo.title: So Long a Letter
volumeInfo.description: An epistolary novel about love and tradition in Senegal.

volumeInfo.title: God's Bits of Wood
volumeInfo.description: A novel about a railway strike in colonial West Africa.

volumeInfo.title: Hopscotch
volumeInfo.description: An experimental novel about love and existentialism.

volumeInfo.title: The Tunnel
volumeInfo.description: A psychological novel about obsession.

volumeInfo.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
volumeInfo.description: A landmark novel of magical realism.

volumeInfo.title: Love in the Time of Cholera
volumeInfo.description: A decades-spanning love story.

volumeInfo.title: The Great Divide
volumeInfo.description: A powerful novel about the construction of the Panama Canal, casting light on the unsung people who lived, loved, and labored there

volumeInfo.title: Luna Verde
volumeInfo.description: A novel about social injustice in Panama.

volumeInfo.title: Cloudstreet
volumeInfo.description: A story of two working-class Australian families.

volumeInfo.title: Picnic at Hanging Rock
volumeInfo.description: A mysterious disappearance in rural Australia.

volumeInfo.title: Midnight's Children
volumeInfo.description: A magical realist novel set during India's independence.

volumeInfo.title: The God of Small Things
volumeInfo.description: A story of family and forbidden love in Kerala.

volumeInfo.title: Fado
volumeInfo.description: Inspired by Jack Kerouac s On the Road, Andrzej Stasiuk, Poland s most accomplished living prose writer, takes readers into the forgotten Europe.

volumeInfo.title: Drive Your Plow Over the Bones of the Dead
volumeInfo.description: A literary mystery set in rural Poland.

volumeInfo.title: Hunger
volumeInfo.description: A psychological novel of urban alienation.

volumeInfo.title: The Ice Palace
volumeInfo.description: Commonly seen as the legendary Norwegian writer's masterpiece, this story tells the tale of Siss and Unn, two friends.

volumeInfo.title: Crime and Punishment
volumeInfo.description: A psychological exploration of guilt and redemption.

volumeInfo.title: War and Peace
volumeInfo.description: An epic novel set during the Napoleonic wars.

volumeInfo.title: Fieldwork in Ukrainian Sex
volumeInfo.description: A feminist novel exploring identity and relationships.

volumeInfo.title: Sweet Darusya
volumeInfo.description: A tragic story set in rural Ukraine.

volumeInfo.title: To Live
volumeInfo.description: A powerful novel following one man's endurance through decades of political and social upheaval in 20th-century China.

volumeInfo.title: The Three-Body Problem
volumeInfo.description: A science fiction epic where first contact with an alien civilization reshapes humanity's future.

volumeInfo.title: The Odyssey
volumeInfo.description: Odyssey is literature's grandest evocation of everyman's journey though life.

volumeInfo.title: Zorba the Greek
volumeInfo.description: The classic novel Zorba the Greek is the story of two men, their incredible friendship, and the importance of living life to the fullest.

volumeInfo.title: Madonna in a Fur Coat
volumeInfo.description: A shy young man leaves his home in rural Turkey to learn a trade in 1920s Berlin.

volumeInfo.title: My Name is Red
volumeInfo.description: Part fantasy and part philosophical puzzle, My Name is Red is a kaleidoscopic journey to the intersection of art, religion, love, sex and power.

volumeInfo.title: Independent People
volumeInfo.description: Having spent eighteen years in humiliating servitude, Bjartur wants nothing more than to raise his flocks unbeholden to any man. But Bjartur's spirited daughter wants to live unbeholden to him.

volumeInfo.title: The Greenhouse
volumeInfo.description: A story about the heartfelt search for beauty in life,  The Greenhouse is a touching reminder of our ability to turn the small things in everyday life into the extraordinary.
